text.title: Strategy Analysis — Current State — Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every application of Strategy Analysis — Current State must enforce.

rule.statement: Every claim about current state cites at least one evidence source (interview note, log sample, screenshot, CMDB row).
rule.rationale: Hearsay-based as-is specs encode whoever's loudest, not what actually happens.

rule.statement: Every process, system, and capability row names an owner.
rule.rationale: Anonymous rows produce un-validatable specs; the owner is the verifier on review.

rule.statement: Every pain point is quantified (frequency × impact) using a defined rubric.
rule.rationale: Qualitative pain ranking is opinion-laundering; the rubric forces comparability.

rule.statement: Constraints are categorised into regulatory / contractual / technical / cultural.
rule.rationale: Uncategorised constraints get treated equally; categorisation drives the right escalation path.

rule.statement: Process maps go max 3 levels deep; deeper is redundant for strategy analysis.
rule.rationale: Beyond level 3, granularity is implementation detail that belongs in requirements, not strategy.
